assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: In plan.md Step 4 ('Understand the Code'), the plan explicitly lists inspecting all 4 of the named files: 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/get.rs -- sibling endpoint handler pattern', 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs -- understand existing fetch, list methods', 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs -- understand AdvisorySummary struct and its severity field', and 'common/src/error.rs -- understand AppError pattern'. All four are referenced.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan's Files Overview (plan.md lines 162-173) lists 7 files. Files 1-5 and 7 match the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create. However, File 6 is 'docs/api.md' with action MODIFY, which is not listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create sections. Although file-6-description.md states this is 'conditional', it is still listed as a planned modification in the Files Overview table, making it an out-of-scope file modification.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Files Overview table lists: File 1 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs' (CREATE) — the severity_summary model; File 4 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs' (CREATE) — the severity_summary endpoint handler; File 7 'tests/api/advisory_summary.rs' (CREATE) — the integration test file. All three Files to Create are present.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Files Overview table lists: File 3 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs' (MODIFY); File 5 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs' (MODIFY); File 2 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs' (MODIFY). All three Files to Modify are present.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 10 shows the commit message: 'feat(api): add advisory severity aggregation endpoint' which follows Conventional Commits format with type 'feat', scope '(api)', and description. The footer contains 'Implements TC-9201' which references the Jira issue.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 10 explicitly states: 'With `--trailer="Assisted-by: Claude Code"`.' directly after the commit message block.

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 5 shows: 'git checkout main', 'git pull', 'git checkout -b TC-9201'. This creates a branch named TC-9201 off of 'main'. Step 10 also shows 'gh pr create --base main' confirming the target branch is 'main'. Step 1 also parses 'Target Branch: main' from the task.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md explicitly documents both patterns: (1) Error handling section states 'All endpoint handlers return Result<T, AppError>' and 'Errors are wrapped with .context() from the AppError enum in common/src/error.rs'; (2) Module structure section states 'Every domain module follows the model/ + service/ + endpoints/ tripartite structure' with detailed sub-patterns for each.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 1 explicitly lists 'Target Branch: main' as a parsed section from TC-9201. Step 5 shows 'git checkout main' as the base for the new branch. Step 10 shows 'gh pr create --base main'.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 1.5 ('Verify Description Integrity') states: 'Would fetch comments from TC-9201 and look for [sdlc-workflow] Description digest: marker. Compare digest if found. (Simulated -- skip in eval.)' While this mentions checking for the digest marker, it does NOT mention the backward-compatible behavior of proceeding with a warning when no digest is found. It only says the step is simulated/skipped for eval, which is not equivalent to documenting the warning-and-proceed behavior.
